description: A synchronous FIFO (First-In-First-Out) buffer with configurable data width and depth that provides standard FIFO operations with status flags

modules.name: sync_fifo
modules.description: Synchronous FIFO with parameterizable depth and data width

parameters.name: DATA_WIDTH
parameters.description: Width of data bus in bits

parameters.name: DEPTH
parameters.description: FIFO depth in number of entries

ports.name: count
ports.description: Current number of entries in FIFO

requirements.description: Data shall be read in the same order it was written (first-in-first-out)
The full flag shall be asserted when FIFO contains DEPTH entries and shall prevent further writes
The empty flag shall be asserted when FIFO contains 0 entries and shall prevent further reads
Write operations shall be ignored when full is asserted (unless simultaneous read)
Read operations shall be ignored when empty is asserted (unless simultaneous write)
The count signal shall accurately reflect the number of entries in the FIFO
The almost_full flag shall be asserted when count equals (DEPTH-1)
The almost_empty flag shall be asserted when count equals 1
When wr_en and rd_en are both asserted: if empty perform write only, if full perform read only, otherwise perform both
Upon rst_n assertion: FIFO cleared, empty=1, full=0, count=0, pointers reset to 0
All outputs shall be synchronous to the rising edge of clk
Data shall not be corrupted during storage or retrieval

corner_cases.how_to_test: Fill FIFO completely then attempt additional writes, verify writes are ignored and no data corruption occurs
Empty FIFO completely then attempt additional reads, verify reads are ignored and output remains stable
Fill FIFO then assert both wr_en and rd_en, verify only read operation occurs
Empty FIFO then assert both wr_en and rd_en, verify only write operation occurs
Fill and empty FIFO multiple times to exercise pointer wraparound at depth boundaries
Rapidly alternate between filling and emptying FIFO to test flag stability
Test behavior when FIFO has exactly 1 entry, verify almost_empty flag and transitions
Assert reset during active read/write operations, verify clean recovery to reset state
Perform consecutive read/write operations without idle cycles, verify correct behavior
Test with various data patterns (all 0s, all 1s, random, walking bits) to verify data integrity
